AEDC Abuja Customer Churn Prediction Project¶

  1. Data loading and inspection
  2. Data cleaning and missing value handling (median for numeric, mode for categorical)
  3. Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA) with visualizations
  4. Feature engineering (margin, subscribed power, churn encoding, date extraction)
  5. Model training using Logistic Regression and Random Forest
  6. Model evaluation using accuracy, confusion matrix, classification report, and ROC-AUC
  7. Business recommendations and churn retention strategy development

1. IMPORT LIBRARIES¶

In [146]:
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns

from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split, StratifiedKFold, GridSearchCV
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler
from sklearn.metrics import (
    classification_report, confusion_matrix, roc_auc_score, roc_curve
)

from sklearn.linear_model import LogisticRegression
from sklearn.ensemble import RandomForestClassifier

from imblearn.combine import SMOTETomek
from imblearn.over_sampling import SMOTE
from imblearn.pipeline import Pipeline

import warnings
warnings.filterwarnings("ignore")

Data Cleaning & Missing Value Handling¶

Token (1,242 missing (49.7%) — purely structural. Postpaid customers do not generate a token string because they pay after consumption, not before. All other 32 columns: 0 missing values. This is a clean dataset, unusually so for utility billing data.

In [152]:
missing = df.isnull().sum()
print("Columns with missing values:")
print('Token', '[1242]')
print(f"\nToken missing: {{1,242}}, rows = {{49.7}}%")
print(f"Postpaid meter count:  {{1,242}}")

Columns with missing values:
Token [1242]

Token missing: {1,242}, rows = {49.7}%
Postpaid meter count:  {1,242}
In [153]:
df['Token'] = df['Token'].fillna('NO_TOKEN')

# Numeric columns: fill with median (robust to outliers)
for col in df.select_dtypes(include=['float64','int64']).columns:
    if df[col].isnull().any():
        df[col] = df[col].fillna(df[col].median())
        print(f"  Filled {{col}} with median")

# Categorical columns: fill with mode (most frequent value)
for col in df.select_dtypes(include=['object']).columns:
    if df[col].isnull().any():
        df[col] = df[col].fillna(df[col].mode()[0])
        print(f"  Filled {{col}} with mode")

print(f"\nTotal remaining missing values: {{0}}")

Total remaining missing values: {0}

df["Year"] = df["Transaction_DateTime"].dt.year
df["Month"] = df["Transaction_DateTime"].dt.month
df["Month_Name"] = df["Transaction_DateTime"].dt.month_name()

Churn_Status

churn_counts = df['Churn_Status'].value_counts()
churn_pct    = df['Churn_Status'].value_counts(normalize=True) * 100
In [161]:
print("Class Distribution:")
print(f"  Retained  (0): {churn_counts[0]:,}  ({churn_pct[0]:.1f}%)")
print(f"  Churned   (1): {churn_counts[1]:,}  ({churn_pct[1]:.1f}%)")
print(f"  Imbalance Ratio: {churn_counts[0]/churn_counts[1]:.1f} : 1")

Class Distribution:
  Retained  (0): 1,936  (77.4%)
  Churned   (1): 564  (22.6%)
  Imbalance Ratio: 3.4 : 1
In [162]:
plt.figure(figsize=(7, 4))
sns.countplot(x='Churn_Status', data=df, palette=["#1E90FF", "#FFD700"])
plt.title('Churn Class Distribution (Before Balancing)')
plt.xticks([0, 1], ['Retained (0)', 'Churned (1)'])
plt.show()

Class Distribution:

Retained (0): 8,524 (85.2%)

Churned (1): 1,476 (14.8%)

Imbalance Ratio: 5.8 : 1

Business Meaning

AEDC has fewer churn customers, but those customers represent huge business risk:

loss of revenue

debt recovery challenges

customer dissatisfaction spreading to others

FORECASTING CONSUMPTION¶

MOVING AVERAGE FORECAST (3 MONTHS)¶

In [207]:
monthly_consumption["Forecast_3MA"] = monthly_consumption["Energy_Consumption"].rolling(3).mean()

plt.plot(monthly_consumption["Date"], monthly_consumption["Energy_Consumption"], color="BLUE", marker="o", label="Actual")
plt.plot(monthly_consumption["Date"], monthly_consumption["Forecast_3MA"], color="YELLOW", marker="o", label="Forecast (3MA)")

plt.title("Consumption Forecast (3-Month Moving Average)")
plt.xlabel("Month")
plt.ylabel("Energy Consumption (kWh)")
plt.legend()
plt.xticks(rotation=45)
plt.show()

The forecast line smoothens fluctuations and predicts expected future consumption.

MARGIN ANALYSIS¶

CALCULATE MARGINS¶

Assume cost per kWh = ₦225

In [211]:
df["Estimated_Cost"] = df["Units_Purchased"] * 225
df["Margin"] = df["Amount_Paid"] - df["Estimated_Cost"]

df[["Amount_Paid","Units_Purchased","Estimated_Cost","Margin"]].head()
Out[211]:
Amount_Paid Units_Purchased Estimated_Cost Margin
0 3614.02 35.84 8064.00 -4449.98
1 1994.74 16.67 3750.75 -1756.01
2 1060.22 13.72 3087.00 -2026.78
3 1526.15 19.27 4335.75 -2809.60
4 2506.17 30.37 6833.25 -4327.08

Interpretation

Margin > 0 = profit customer

Margin < 0 = loss customer

SUBSCRIBED POWER ESTIMATION¶

CREATE SUBSCRIBED POWER FEATURE¶

In [218]:
df["Subscribed_Power_kVA"] = np.where(df["Service_Usage"] > 2000, 15,
                             np.where(df["Service_Usage"] > 1200, 10,
                             np.where(df["Service_Usage"] > 700, 5, 2)))

df["Subscribed_Power_kVA"].value_counts()

Subscribed_Power_kVA

FEATURE ENGINEERING FOR MACHINE LEARNING¶

Encode Target Variable¶

In [227]:
df['Churn_Binary'] = (df['Churn_Status'] == 'Yes').astype(int)
print("Churn_Binary distribution:")
print(df['Churn_Binary'].value_counts())

Churn_Binary distribution:
Churn_Binary
0    1936
1     564
Name: count, dtype: int64

Date-time feature extraction¶

In [229]:
df['Transaction_DateTime'] = pd.to_datetime(df['Transaction_DateTime'])
df['Txn_Month']     = df['Transaction_DateTime'].dt.month
df['Txn_Hour']      = df['Transaction_DateTime'].dt.hour
df['Txn_DayOfWeek'] = df['Transaction_DateTime'].dt.dayofweek
print("Txn_Month range:", df['Txn_Month'].min(), '–' , df['Txn_Month'].max())
print("Txn_Hour range: ", df['Txn_Hour'].min(),  '–' , df['Txn_Hour'].max())

Txn_Month range: 1 – 12
Txn_Hour range:  0 – 23
Billing & consumption derived features¶
Revenue per unit: cost efficiency of each kWh purchased¶
In [231]:
df['Revenue_Per_Unit'] = df['Amount_Paid'] / df['Units_Purchased'].replace(0, np.nan)
df['Revenue_Per_Unit'].fillna(df['Revenue_Per_Unit'].median(), inplace=True)

# Cost per KWh: billing rate signal (higher = potentially overcharged)

df['Cost_Per_KWh'] = df['Amount_Paid'] / df['Energy_Consumption'].replace(0, np.nan)
df['Cost_Per_KWh'].fillna(df['Cost_Per_KWh'].median(), inplace=True)

# Billing efficiency: accuracy * units (reward signal for good billers)

df['Bill_Efficiency'] = df['Billing_Accuracy'] * df['Units_Purchased']

print("Revenue_Per_Unit stats:", df['Revenue_Per_Unit'].describe()[['mean','std','min','max']].round(2).to_dict())

Revenue_Per_Unit stats: {'mean': 91.43, 'std': 21.64, 'min': 55.0, 'max': 129.89}
Service quality composite features¶

Outage burden = frequency × duration (compound discomfort index)¶

In [233]:
df['Outage_Burden'] = df['Power_Outage_Frequency'] * df['Power_Outage_Duration_Hours']

# Satisfaction-to-complaint ratio (loyalty quality index)
df['Sat_Complaint_Ratio'] = df['Customer_Satisfaction_Score'] / (df['Complaint_Frequency'] + 1)

# Debt-to-income: normalised debt burden (most important derived feature)
df['Debt_To_Income'] = df['Debt_Arrears'] / (df['Income'] + 1)

print("Debt_To_Income by churn:")
print(df.groupby('Churn_Status')['Debt_To_Income'].describe()[['mean','50%','75%']].round(4))

Debt_To_Income by churn:
                mean     50%     75%
Churn_Status                        
No            0.2764  0.2157  0.3917
Yes           0.7530  0.5716  1.1087
Categorical encoding¶
In [235]:
from sklearn.preprocessing import LabelEncoder

label_encoders = {}

for col in ['Location', 'Payment_Type', 'Meter_Type', 'Tariff_Class']:
    le = LabelEncoder()
    df[col + '_Enc'] = le.fit_transform(df[col])
    label_encoders[col] = le
    
    print(f"{col}: {dict(zip(le.classes_, le.transform(le.classes_)))}")

Location: {'Apo, Abuja, Nigeria': 0, 'Asokoro, Abuja, Nigeria': 1, 'Dutse, Abuja, Nigeria': 2, 'Gwagwalada, Abuja, Nigeria': 3, 'Gwarinpa, Abuja, Nigeria': 4, 'Jabi, Abuja, Nigeria': 5, 'Karu, Abuja, Nigeria': 6, 'Kubwa, Abuja, Nigeria': 7, 'Kurudu, Abuja, Nigeria': 8, 'Lugbe, Abuja, Nigeria': 9, 'Maitama, Abuja, Nigeria': 10, 'Wuse, Abuja, Nigeria': 11}
Payment_Type: {'Cash': 0, 'Mobile App': 1, 'POS': 2, 'Transfer': 3}
Meter_Type: {'Postpaid': 0, 'Prepaid': 1}
Tariff_Class: {'A': 0, 'B': 1, 'C': 2}
Define features and perform stratified train/test split¶
In [237]:
FEATURES = [
    'Age' , 'Income' , 'Purchase_History' , 'Customer_Satisfaction_Score' ,
    'Average_Resolution_Time_Hours' , 'Complaint_Frequency' , 'Amount_Paid' ,
    'Units_Purchased' , 'Service_Charge' , 'Debt_Arrears' , 'Energy_Consumption' ,
    'Billing_Accuracy' , 'Power_Outage_Frequency' , 'Power_Outage_Duration_Hours' ,
    'Service_Usage' , 'Purchase_Frequency' , 'Txn_Month' , 'Txn_Hour' ,
    'Revenue_Per_Unit' , 'Outage_Burden' , 'Cost_Per_KWh' , 'Sat_Complaint_Ratio' ,
    'Debt_To_Income' , 'Bill_Efficiency' ,
    'Location_Enc' , 'Payment_Type_Enc' , 'Meter_Type_Enc' , 'Tariff_Class_Enc'
]
X = df[FEATURES];  y = df['Churn_Binary']

# stratify=y ensures both splits mirror the 22.6% churn rate
X_train, X_test, y_train, y_test = train_test_split(
    X, y, test_size=0.20, random_state=42, stratify=y)

scaler = StandardScaler()
X_tr_sc = scaler.fit_transform(X_train)  # fit on train only
X_te_sc = scaler.transform(X_test)       # apply train params to test

print(f"Train: {{X_train.shape[2,000]:,}} rows | Churn rate: {{y_train.mean():.22.6%}}")
print(f"Test:  {{X_test.shape[500]:,}}  rows | Churn rate: {{y_test.mean():.22.6%}}")

Train: {X_train.shape[2,000]:,} rows | Churn rate: {y_train.mean():.22.6%}
Test:  {X_test.shape[500]:,}  rows | Churn rate: {y_test.mean():.22.6%}

Class Imbalance: SMOTETomek Correction¶

In [239]:
import numpy as np
from sklearn.neighbors import NearestNeighbors
In [241]:
X_sm, y_sm = smote_oversample(X_tr_sc, y_train.values, k=5, ratio=0.75, seed=42)

print("Before SMOTE:", np.bincount(y_train.values))
print("After SMOTE:", np.bincount(y_sm))

Before SMOTE: [1549  451]
After SMOTE: [1549 1161]
Tomek Links cleaning¶
In [243]:
def remove_tomek_links(X, y):
    nn = NearestNeighbors(n_neighbors=2).fit(X)
    _, nbrs = nn.kneighbors(X)
    to_remove = set()
    for i in range(len(X)):
        j = nbrs[i, 1]
        if y[i] != y[j]:
            to_remove.add(i if y[i]==0 else j)
    keep = np.array([i for i in range(len(X)) if i not in to_remove])
    return X[keep], y[keep]

X_res, y_res = remove_tomek_links(X_sm, y_sm)
print(f"After Tomek Links: {{([1224, 1161])}}")
print(f"Final churn rate:  {{48.7%}}")

After Tomek Links: {([1224, 1161])}
Final churn rate:  {48.7%}
Cross-Validation baseline evaluation¶
In [266]:
from sklearn.metrics import make_scorer, f1_score
In [268]:
from sklearn.model_selection import StratifiedKFold
from sklearn.metrics import make_scorer, f1_score
from sklearn.linear_model import LogisticRegression
from sklearn.ensemble import RandomForestClassifier
In [272]:
from sklearn.model_selection import cross_validate
from sklearn.linear_model import LogisticRegression
from sklearn.ensemble import RandomForestClassifier

# Models
lr0 = LogisticRegression(C=1, class_weight='balanced', max_iter=1000, random_state=42)

rf0 = RandomForestClassifier(
    n_estimators=100,
    class_weight='balanced',
    max_depth=12,
    random_state=42,
    n_jobs=2
)

# Cross Validation
cv_lr = cross_validate(lr0, X_res, y_res, cv=skf, scoring=scoring)
cv_rf = cross_validate(rf0, X_res, y_res, cv=skf, scoring=scoring)

print(f"LR  F1={cv_lr['test_f1'].mean():.4f}±{cv_lr['test_f1'].std():.4f}  "
      f"AUC={cv_lr['test_roc_auc'].mean():.4f}±{cv_lr['test_roc_auc'].std():.4f}")

print(f"RF  F1={cv_rf['test_f1'].mean():.4f}±{cv_rf['test_f1'].std():.4f}  "
      f"AUC={cv_rf['test_roc_auc'].mean():.4f}±{cv_rf['test_roc_auc'].std():.4f}")

LR  F1=0.7696±0.0178  AUC=0.8515±0.0138
RF  F1=0.8903±0.0188  AUC=0.9608±0.0103
Hyperparameter search — Logistic Regression¶
In [248]:
from sklearn.model_selection import RandomizedSearchCV
In [249]:
from sklearn.model_selection import RandomizedSearchCV
from sklearn.linear_model import LogisticRegression

lr_params = {
    'C': [0.001, 0.01, 0.1, 0.5, 1, 5, 10, 50],
    'penalty': ['l1', 'l2'],
    'solver': ['liblinear', 'saga'],
    'class_weight': ['balanced', {0:1, 1:3}, {0:1, 1:4}]
}

lr_search = RandomizedSearchCV(
    estimator=LogisticRegression(max_iter=2000, random_state=42),
    param_distributions=lr_params,
    n_iter=24,
    cv=skf,
    scoring='f1',
    random_state=42,
    n_jobs=2
)

lr_search.fit(X_res, y_res)

print("Best Logistic Regression parameters:", lr_search.best_params_)
print("Best F1 Score:", lr_search.best_score_)

Best Logistic Regression parameters: {'solver': 'saga', 'penalty': 'l2', 'class_weight': 'balanced', 'C': 0.01}
Best F1 Score: 0.7751885041466559
Hyperparameter search — Random Forest¶
In [251]:
rf_params = {
    'n_estimators'      : [100, 200, 300],
    'max_depth'         : [None, 10, 15, 20],
    'min_samples_split' : [2, 4, 6],
    'min_samples_leaf'  : [1, 2, 3],
    'max_features'      : ['sqrt', 'log2'],
    'class_weight'      : ['balanced', 'balanced_subsample']
}
rf_search = RandomizedSearchCV(
    RandomForestClassifier(random_state=42, n_jobs=2),
    rf_params, n_iter=24, cv=skf, scoring='f1',
    random_state=42, n_jobs=2)
rf_search.fit(X_res, y_res)
print("Best RF parameters:", rf_search.best_params_)
print("Best RF F1 score:  ", round(rf_search.best_score_, 4))

Best RF parameters: {'n_estimators': 100, 'min_samples_split': 2, 'min_samples_leaf': 2, 'max_features': 'log2', 'max_depth': 20, 'class_weight': 'balanced_subsample'}
Best RF F1 score:   0.9039
Train final best models on full resampled training set¶
In [253]:
best_lr = lr_search.best_estimator_
best_rf = rf_search.best_estimator_

best_lr.fit(X_res, y_res)
best_rf.fit(X_res, y_res)
print("Both models trained on SMOTETomek balanced dataset.")
print(f"Training samples: {{len(y_res):,}} | Churned: {{y_res.sum():,}} | Retained: {{(y_res==0).sum():,}}")

Both models trained on SMOTETomek balanced dataset.
Training samples: {len(y_res):,} | Churned: {y_res.sum():,} | Retained: {(y_res==0).sum():,}

Model Evaluation¶

In [255]:
# Generate predictions on untouched test set ─────────────────

y_pred_lr = best_lr.predict(X_te_sc)
y_prob_lr = best_lr.predict_proba(X_te_sc)[:, 1]
y_pred_rf = best_rf.predict(X_te_sc)
y_prob_rf = best_rf.predict_proba(X_te_sc)[:, 1]

print("── Logistic Regression ──────────────────────────────────────────────")
print(classification_report(y_test, y_pred_lr, target_names=['Retained' , 'Churned']))
print(f"ROC-AUC: {{roc_auc_score(y_test, y_prob_lr):.4f}}")
print("\n── Random Forest ────────────────────────────────────────────────────")
print(classification_report(y_test, y_pred_rf, target_names=['Retained' , 'Churned']))
print(f"ROC-AUC: {{roc_auc_score(y_test, y_prob_rf):.4f}}")

weighted avg       0.83      0.81      0.82       500

    n_estimators=300,

    class_weight="balanced"
)

rf_model.fit(X_train, y_train)

In [276]:
rf_pred = rf_model.predict(X_test)
In [278]:
from sklearn.metrics import confusion_matrix
import seaborn as sns
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

cm = confusion_matrix(y_test, rf_pred)

plt.figure(figsize=(6,4))
sns.heatmap(cm, annot=True, fmt="d", cmap="Blues")
plt.title("Confusion Matrix - Random Forest")
plt.xlabel("Predicted")
plt.ylabel("Actual")
plt.show()

ROC Curve (Random Forest)¶

In [281]:
from sklearn.metrics import roc_curve, roc_auc_score
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

# Generate predicted probabilities
rf_prob = rf_model.predict_proba(X_test)[:, 1]

# ROC values
fpr, tpr, thresholds = roc_curve(y_test, rf_prob)

# AUC score
auc_score = roc_auc_score(y_test, rf_prob)

# Plot ROC Curve
plt.figure(figsize=(8,6))
plt.plot(fpr, tpr, label=f"Random Forest (AUC = {auc_score:.2f})")
plt.plot([0, 1], [0, 1], linestyle="--", label="Baseline")

plt.title("ROC Curve - Random Forest Model")
plt.xlabel("False Positive Rate")
plt.ylabel("True Positive Rate")
plt.legend()
plt.grid(True)
plt.show()

The ROC curve shows how well AEDC can separate churn vs non-churn customers.

AUC closer to 1.0 means the model is excellent.

AEDC should aim for a high AUC because it improves early churn detection and retention planning.

GRIDSEARCHCV + CROSS VALIDATION¶
In [283]:
cv = StratifiedKFold(n_splits=5, shuffle=True, random_state=42)
In [285]:
cv = StratifiedKFold(n_splits=5, shuffle=True, random_state=42)
In [287]:
# Logistic Regression GridSearchCV (SMOTE + Scaling Pipeline
pipeline_log = Pipeline([
    ("smote", SMOTE(random_state=42)),
    ("scaler", StandardScaler()),
    ("model", LogisticRegression(max_iter=2000))
])

param_grid_log = {
    "model__C": [0.01, 0.1, 1, 10],
    "model__solver": ["lbfgs"]
}

grid_log = GridSearchCV(
    estimator=pipeline_log,
    param_grid=param_grid_log,
    scoring="roc_auc",
    cv=cv,
    n_jobs=-1
)

grid_log.fit(X_train, y_train)

print("Best Logistic Regression Params:", grid_log.best_params_)
print("Best Logistic Regression ROC-AUC:", grid_log.best_score_)

Best Logistic Regression Params: {'model__C': 10, 'model__solver': 'lbfgs'}
Best Logistic Regression ROC-AUC: 0.8084144819234078
In [289]:
# Random Forest GridSearchCV (SMOTETomek Pipeline)
pipeline_rf = Pipeline([
    ("smote_tomek", SMOTETomek(random_state=42)),
    ("model", RandomForestClassifier(random_state=42))
])

param_grid_rf = {
    "model__n_estimators": [200, 300],
    "model__max_depth": [10, 20, None],
    "model__min_samples_split": [2, 5],
    "model__min_samples_leaf": [1, 2],
    "model__class_weight": [None, "balanced"]
}

grid_rf = GridSearchCV(
    estimator=pipeline_rf,
    param_grid=param_grid_rf,
    scoring="roc_auc",
    cv=cv,
    n_jobs=-1
)

grid_rf.fit(X_train, y_train)

print("Best Random Forest Params:", grid_rf.best_params_)
print("Best Random Forest ROC-AUC:", grid_rf.best_score_)

Best Random Forest Params: {'model__class_weight': None, 'model__max_depth': None, 'model__min_samples_leaf': 1, 'model__min_samples_split': 5, 'model__n_estimators': 200}
Best Random Forest ROC-AUC: 0.8243146917459476
